title: WIP: Relax locking on container exec
body:
At present, we lock until the exec'd process exits. That is far too aggressive, and blocks any other API operation while an exec session is running. Only lock while we're checking the status of the container.

There may be issues around cleanup caused by this. We may need more active tracking of exec sessions to make sure we don't clean up storage while an exec is running.

comments.body: DO NOT MERGE YET, I think I've exposed an issue with container cleanup

To summarize the issue, if we don't guarantee everything is locked until exec finishes, the container can be unmounted while exec is still running.

To avoid this we need to track exec sessions and refuse to unmount if things there are active exec sessions.
Wouldn't the unmount be blocked merely by a process running in the container?

@rhatdan Also applies to remove, including rm -f, which would not be able to remove the container because something we don't know about is using the rootfs. I don't think we can get away with not tracking these.
FWIW Homu will ignore requests to merge PRs starting with `WIP:`.
@rhatdan I've ended up having to pull apart the exec code and put it back together. Hopefully get this in tomorrow.
